title: Hidden-dependency mapping across Erdos combinatorics conjectures
overarching_problem: Map the hidden dependency structures shared by open Erdos problems in graph Ramsey theory and additive combinatorics, with emphasis on which assumptions trigger recurring lemmas, which perturbations shift the apparent provability boundary, and which formalizations expose reusable infrastructure across the family.
success_criteria: Surface recurring lemmas that reappear across more than one conjecture or more than one experimental move
Map which explicit assumptions appear structurally critical versus merely search-sensitive
Identify boundary points where weakening or reformulating a statement changes the blocker classification
Produce reusable hidden-dependency sketches that transfer between graph-theoretic and additive-combinatorial variants
Identify at least one previously unknown intermediate lemma backed by Lean artifacts
Produce a counterexample witness or independence argument for at least one weakened variant
non_goals: Do not optimize for proving the original conjecture outright
Do not treat failed or stalled runs as evidence of falsity
Do not drift outside combinatorics, graph theory, and additive combinatorics
